content.problemStatement:
Write a program to check if a given array of integers is sorted in increasing order.

An array is considered to be sorted in increasing order if each element is greater than the previous element, except for the first element.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single positive number NNN - the length of array.
The second line of input contains NNN space separated numbers - the elements of the array.


Output Format

Output on a single line, `YES` if the array is sorted, else `NO`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array; the second line contains N space‑separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing "YES" if the array is strictly increasing, otherwise print "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array 3 4 9 1 7 2 is not strictly increasing because 1 is not greater than 9.
Each element is larger than its predecessor, so the array is strictly increasing.
A single‑element array is trivially strictly increasing.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the array from left to right keeping the previous element. If the current element is less than or equal to the previous one the array is not strictly increasing. Otherwise we continue. After processing all elements we output YES if no violation was found, else NO. This linear scan is optimal because every element must be examined at least once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int t=0;t<T;t++){int N=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());long prev=0;boolean first=true;boolean sorted=true;for(int i=0;i<N;i++){long cur=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken());if(first){prev=cur;first=false;}else{if(cur<=prev) sorted=false;prev=cur;}}sb.append(sorted?"YES":"NO");if(t<T-1) sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
